questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a core feature of Java?
What is the size of an 'int' variable in Java?
Which component is responsible for executing Java byte code?
What is the root class of all classes in Java?
Which keyword is used to establish inheritance between classes?
What happens when you apply the 'final' keyword to a variable?
options: Its value cannot be changed once assigned
It can only be accessed within its class
It is stored in the heap memory
questions.answer: Its value cannot be changed once assigned

questions.text: Which collection class is used to store key-value pairs?
What is the default value of a boolean variable in Java?
Can you overload the main() method in a Java class?
options: Only if it returns an int
Creating multiple forms of a method
Wrapping code and data together into a single unit
Creating a new class from an existing class
questions.answer: Wrapping code and data together into a single unit

questions.text: Which block is ALWAYS executed in a try-catch structure, regardless of whether an exception occurs?
Which method must be invoked to begin the execution of a new Thread?
What is the purpose of the 'transient' keyword?
options: To mark a variable as thread-safe
To prevent a variable from being serialized
To make a variable accessible across packages
To indicate a variable is temporary in memory
questions.answer: To prevent a variable from being serialized

questions.text: What type of exception is a NullPointerException?
